entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The crop Cicer arietinum L. being studied for genetic diversity and heterosis

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The tendency of a crossbred individual to show qualities superior to those of both parents

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species or population

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERGENCE
entities.description: The process of populations accumulating genetic differences, measured here by D2 values

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the crop, specifically seed yield

entities.name: EARLINESS
entities.description: The trait referring to the time taken for the plant to reach maturity

entities.name: NUMBER OF PODS PER PLANT
entities.description: A yield attribute measuring the quantity of pods produced per plant

entities.name: SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the seeds produced by the plant

entities.name: NUMBER OF PRIMARY BRANCHES PER PLANT
entities.description: A morphological trait of the chickpea plant

entities.name: D2 VALUES
entities.description: A statistical method used to measure genetic distance or divergence between parents

entities.name: GENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The overall scientific approach used to study the genetic variation and heterosis in the crop

relationships.description: Genetic analysis was carried out on chickpea lines
Study of heterosis in relation to genetic diversity in chickpea
Study of genetic diversity in chickpea
Parents separated by high to medium genetic divergence generally showed higher heterosis
Heterosis plays a role in realizing higher yield in chickpea
Genetic diversity of parents plays a role in realizing higher yield in chickpea
Significant mid parent heterosis was observed for earliness
Significant positive mid parent heterosis was observed for number of pods per plant
Significant positive mid parent heterosis was observed for seed weight
Significant positive mid parent heterosis was observed for seed yield
D2 values are used to measure the magnitude of genetic divergence
